origin.name: Mongolia
origin.key_document: Death certificate in Mongolian (requires certified English translation)
origin.source: FCDO Mongolia travel advice, updated 2025; British Embassy Ulaanbaatar guidance
origin.cremation_note: Cremation in Mongolia is available. Bringing ashes home to the UK is an option alongside full body repatriation.

destinations.name: United Kingdom
key_documents: Death certificate in Mongolian (certified English translation required)
special_rules: All Mongolian-language documentation requires a certified English translation for UK acceptance.
Police clearance is required for all violent or suspicious deaths.
An export permit from Mongolian authorities is required for all repatriations.
Embalming must be carried out by a licensed funeral director before air transport.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All Mongolian documentation must be in certified English translation. The coroner for the district is notified. Straightforward cases proceed directly to funeral arrangements.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. The British Embassy in Ulaanbaatar can register the death and advise on local funeral directors. They cannot pay for or arrange repatriation.
destinations.seo_title: Mongolia to UK Repatriation: Guidance for Families
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Mongolia. Repatriation to the UK takes 2-3 weeks. State Registration, Mongolian translation, and export permits all handled. Contact us 24/7.
